description: The Loomis Gang was a family of outlaws operating in Central New York during the mid-19th century. Founded by George Washington Loomis Sr. and his wife Rhoda Mallet, the gang specialized in horse theft, livestock rustling, counterfeiting, and burglary. They expanded into a multi-state syndicate under Wash Loomis in the 1840s, buying protection from authorities and cultivating local goodwill to avoid prosecution.

country_links.evidence_quote: Bill Loomis managed the northern region in Hastings Center; he directed a ring of horse thieves and sold the horses in Canada.
country_links.context: The Loomis Gang sold stolen horses in Canada.
